```{=html}
  <details class="plate">
    <summary><span class="plate-n">Plate VII</span><span class="plate-title">Bibliography & recommended reading</span></summary>
    <div class="plate-body">
      <p>Dervin's core works, in order of where to start. Citation details below have been checked against targeted public bibliographic records and DOI/library pages; remaining caveats are called out explicitly.</p>
      <div class="smm-callout-box" style="margin-bottom:20px;"><strong>Start here</strong> Dervin, B. (1992). “From the mind’s eye of the user: The Sense-Making qualitative-quantitative methodology.” In J. D. Glazier & R. R. Powell (Eds.), <em>Qualitative Research in Information Management</em> (pp. 61–84). Englewood, CO: Libraries Unlimited. — The most accessible single-paper introduction to the qualitative-quantitative methodology, situational questioning, gaps, and uses/helps. If you read one thing, read this.</div>
      <table class="compare-table">
        <thead><tr><th>Work</th><th>Verified citation</th><th>Use in this course</th></tr></thead>
        <tbody>
          <tr><td><a href="https://philpapers.org/rec/DERIAA" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Dervin (1983)</a></td><td>Dervin, B. (1983). “Information as a user construct: The relevance of perceived information needs to synthesis and interpretation.” In S. A. Ward & L. J. Reed (Eds.), <em>Knowledge Structure and Use: Implications for Synthesis and Interpretation</em> (pp. 155–183). Philadelphia, PA: Temple University Press.</td><td>Foundational statement of information as user construct and perceived information needs. Note: some secondary records list pp. 153–183; PhilPapers lists pp. 155–183.</td></tr>
          <tr><td><a href="https://bibbase.org/network/publication/dervin-fromthemindseyeoftheuserthesensemakingqualitativequantitativemethodology-1992" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Dervin (1992)</a></td><td>Dervin, B. (1992). “From the mind’s eye of the user: The Sense-Making qualitative-quantitative methodology.” In J. D. Glazier & R. R. Powell (Eds.), <em>Qualitative Research in Information Management</em> (pp. 61–84). Englewood, CO: Libraries Unlimited.</td><td>Best single-paper starting point for the qualitative-quantitative methodology, situational questioning, gaps, and uses/helps.</td></tr>
          <tr><td><a href="https://garfield.library.upenn.edu/histcomp/annualreviews/ann-rev-inform-sci-tech/index-tc.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Dervin & Nilan (1986)</a></td><td>Dervin, B., & Nilan, M. (1986). “Information needs and uses.” <em>Annual Review of Information Science and Technology</em>, 21, 3–33.</td><td>Paradigm critique of information needs and uses research.</td></tr>
          <tr><td><a href="https://doi.org/10.1108/13673279810249369" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Dervin (1998)</a></td><td>Dervin, B. (1998). “Sense-making theory and practice: An overview of user interests in knowledge seeking and use.” <em>Journal of Knowledge Management</em>, 2(2), 36–46. https://doi.org/10.1108/13673279810249369</td><td>Sense-making and sense-unmaking for knowledge seeking/use and knowledge-management contexts.</td></tr>
          <tr><td><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/S0306-4573(99)00023-0" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Dervin (1999)</a></td><td>Dervin, B. (1999). “On studying information seeking methodologically: The implications of connecting metatheory to method.” <em>Information Processing & Management</em>, 35(6), 727–750. https://doi.org/10.1016/S0306-4573(99)00023-0</td><td>Methodology / metatheory-to-method article; useful for MMTLI and methodological cautions.</td></tr>
          <tr><td><a href="https://openlibrary.org/books/OL3690333M/Sense-making_methodology_reader" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Dervin, Foreman-Wernet, & Lauterbach (Eds.) (2003)</a></td><td>Dervin, B., Foreman-Wernet, L., & Lauterbach, E. (Eds.). (2003). <em>Sense-Making Methodology Reader: Selected Writings of Brenda Dervin</em>. Cresskill, NJ: Hampton Press. ISBN 1-57273-508-2 / 1-57273-509-0.</td><td>Essential collection. Library records list xv, 397 pages; some retailer records list 384 pages, so library pagination should be preferred.</td></tr>
        </tbody>
      </table>
      <div class="smm-callout-box warn" style="margin-top:20px;"><strong>Citation caveat</strong> For formal academic use, verify Dervin (1983) page range against the physical volume or a library scan. The rest of the core entries now have DOI-backed or library-backed records in the project README.</div>
    </div>
  </details>
```
